all.en: The Green Shift: How Companies Are Embracing Sustainability
all.zh: 綠色轉變：企業如何擁抱永續發展

all.en: Across industries, businesses are rethinking how they operate in response to growing environmental concerns and shifting consumer expectations.
all.zh: 各行各業中，企業正重新思考其營運方式，以回應日益增長的環境疑慮及消費者期待的轉變。

all.en: A recent survey by the Global Business Institute found that 78 percent of large corporations now have formal sustainability strategies in place.
all.zh: 全球商業研究院最近的一項調查發現，78% 的大型企業現已制定正式的永續策略。

all.en: This represents a sharp increase from just 42 percent five years ago.
all.zh: 相較於五年前僅 42%，此數字大幅增加。

all.en: One of the most visible trends is the transition to renewable energy sources for office buildings and manufacturing facilities.
all.zh: 最明顯的趨勢之一是辦公大樓及製造廠轉向再生能源。

all.en: Major retailers such as Northgate Group have pledged to power 100 percent of their operations with solar and wind energy by 2030.
all.zh: Northgate 集團等大型零售商已承諾於 2030 年前以太陽能及風能供應 100% 的營運用電。

all.en: Supply chain transparency has also become a priority for many organizations.
all.zh: 供應鏈透明度也成為許多組織的優先事項。

all.en: Companies are increasingly requiring their suppliers to disclose carbon emissions and labor practices.
all.zh: 企業越來越要求其供應商揭露碳排放及勞工實務。

all.en: Some firms have gone further by publishing detailed annual sustainability reports audited by independent third parties.
all.zh: 部分公司更進一步出版由獨立第三方稽核的詳細年度永續報告。

all.en: Packaging is another area undergoing rapid change.
all.zh: 包裝是另一個快速變革的領域。

all.en: Beverage producers and food manufacturers are replacing single-use plastics with biodegradable or recyclable alternatives.
all.zh: 飲料生產商及食品製造商正將一次性塑膠替換為可生物降解或可回收的替代品。

all.en: 「Consumers, especially younger ones, are willing to pay a premium for environmentally responsible products,」 said industry analyst Rebecca Tanaka.
all.zh: 「消費者，特別是較年輕的族群，願意為對環境負責的產品支付溢價，」產業分析師 Rebecca Tanaka 表示。

all.en: Remote and hybrid work arrangements have additionally contributed to reducing corporate carbon footprints.
all.zh: 遠端及混合工作安排也對減少企業碳足跡有所貢獻。

all.en: Fewer commutes mean lower transportation emissions and reduced energy consumption in office buildings.
all.zh: 通勤減少意味著運輸排放降低，辦公大樓的能源消耗也減少。

all.en: However, experts caution that some sustainability claims should be examined carefully.
all.zh: 然而，專家警告應仔細檢視某些永續性聲明。

all.en: The practice of overstating environmental benefits, sometimes called greenwashing, has prompted regulators in several countries to introduce stricter advertising standards.
all.zh: 誇大環境效益的做法，有時稱為「漂綠」，已促使數個國家的監管機構推出更嚴格的廣告標準。

all.en: Looking ahead, analysts predict that sustainability will move from a marketing differentiator to a core business requirement.
all.zh: 展望未來，分析師預測永續性將從行銷差異化要素轉變為核心業務要求。

all.en: Investors are also paying closer attention, with many funds now incorporating environmental and social criteria into their decisions.
all.zh: 投資人也更加關注，許多基金現已將環境及社會準則納入其決策中。

all.en: For businesses that have yet to act, the message from the market is becoming increasingly clear.
all.zh: 對於尚未採取行動的企業而言，來自市場的訊息正越來越明確。

all.en: Adapting now is no longer optional but essential for long-term competitiveness.
all.zh: 立即適應已不再是選項，而是長期競爭力的必要條件。
